rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HUGH L. HORTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/24/2022 by the assigned judicial officer after review. The following issues must be addressed by an amendment or amended petiiton with new notice: 1) The Court has been requiring a $1,500 minimum cash reserve deemed unavailable for payment of compensation of the conservator and attorneys since the decision of the Conservatorship of A.B. Should the Court adjust the cash reserve upward in light of the recent increase of the Medi-Cal resource limit from $2,000 to $130,000 (as of 07/01/2022)? A specific amount of the cash reserve must be proposed, together with an explanation as to why the proposed amount is appropriate, taking into consideration the conservatee's future cash needs and particular financial circumstances among other factors set forth in Probate Code section 2942(b) and A.B. 2) A specific amount of compensation of the conservator and her attorneys which can be paid out of the estate or set-aside account balance must be proposed, together with an explanation as to why the proposed amount does not impose an economic hardship on the estate, taking into consideration the conservatee's future cash needs and particular financial circumstances among other factors set forth in section 2942(b) and A.B. 3) A specific amount of funds the conservator seeks to set aside to preserve the conservatee's eligibility for public benefits must be proposed, together with an explanation as to why the proposed amount should be set aside in light of the increased Medi-Cal resource limit.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Petitioner Alexander Vaynberg's unopposed Motion for Leave to File Amended Petition is granted. Petitioner is seeking leave to amend his petition to include three new causes of action: (1) that Yafim has failed to properly manage Trust B properties and make them productive; (2) that Respondent has underfunded Trust B by approximately $172,000 (the deficit of which Respondent refuses to correct); and (3) that Respondent failed to account for the Panorama property income from 2016 through its sale in 2020 which Petitioner claims he is owed as one-third owner of the property. Petitioner's argument that judicial policy favors liberal allowances to amend is sufficiently supported by California Civil Procedure Sections 473 and 576 to grant leave. Morgan v. Superior Court (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527 affirms this statutory interpretation and supports granting leave to amend, even after legal proceedings have begun. Petitioner Alexander Vaynberg is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 26, 2022. For remote appearances, see San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUBY MAE HOWARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number has been filed yet. This must be filed confidentially and conventionally. Notice was mailed by the petitioner, who IS an interested party. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Item 3e(1) of the petition indicates that there is a special administrator. There is not. Item 3g(1) of the petition should have been completed, not 3g(2), as appointment of an executor, not an administrator, is being sought. Item 6 of the petition should not be completed when issue survived. Item 8 of the petition is missing the other persons named in the will. They must also receive notice, especially any alternate executor. A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the proposed executor, must be filed to address the inconsistencies in the petition and provide the missing information (parties named in the will, including their relationship to the decedent and address). A courtesy copy of the supplement, the Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities, the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number, and the proof of service of notice mailed to all parties by a disinterested party, and a proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 9/2/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HAROLD K. LIPSET TRUST F/B/O LAWRENCE J. LIPSET
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Trust Due To Changed Circumstances
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Since the settlor amended the trust in 1996, the estate/GST tax exemptions have increased from $600,000/$1,000,000 to $12,060,000. The Court is to determine whether the settlor intended to provide for transfer tax savings for issue of the settlor's child who may become skip persons and whether the increase of the transfer tax exemptions not anticipated by the settlor justifies modification of the sub-trust for Lawrence Lipset under Probate Code section 15409. No explanation has been provided as to why the settlor did not grant any general power of appointment to Lawrence Lipset over his sub-trust upon his death, and why the settlor did not provide for a dynasty trust structure with the special power of appointment and limited distribution given to each generation of issue of Louis Lipset as proposed by this petition, in lieu of outright distribution to Louis Lipset. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIRLEY S.M. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/23/2022. Court calendar full. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Summons not served on Xue Jun Chong or Dolores Chong for the 2014 Will Contest. (See Probate Code 8250 and 8110). Court to determine at eventual hearing whether substituted service on William Chong is proper or should be re-done. 2) Summons not served on Cindy Chong for the 2021 Will Contest. (See Probate Code 8250 and 8110). 3) Examiner does not have a proposed order for William Chong's petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONY ALAN HOLLIFIELD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why counsel has not filed a response to the examiner notes. 2) Examiner Notes: a. Petitioner must file the original will. (Probate Code 8200 et seq.) b. Petitioner must file and 15 days give notice on DE-121. c. Petitioner did not check a required box on page 3 under #5.a. (3) or (4). d. Petitioner did not check a required box on page 3 under #5.a. (7) or (8) e. On petition page 2 petitioner did not fill in #3.d. (7). f. The petition on page 4 #8 does not include the address of Frances Hollifield or date of death. #8 does not include Erin Puruse or Lamona Wood's information. g. Petitioner must file proof of publication. h. Petitioner must file Duties and Liabilities DE-147. i. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. j. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUIS BERT ROVENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) This matter will have to go on for hearing when the other issues are resolved because there appears to be a conflict with the proposed personal representative opening up an estate to sue themselves as personal representative. 2) Petitioner did not select in the caption authorization to administer under the IAEA. 3) Must file Proof of Publication (Probate Code 8120 et seq.) 4) Must give 15 days' notice and file DE-121. 5) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 6) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/15/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
